errors.text: An EED facility near a VHF antenna operating at 100 MHz was assessed. The power density was measured at 7.5 W/m². Table 6-1 specifies a limit of 10 W/m² for the 32–150 MHz band. The measured value is below the threshold and HERO-safe.
errors.reasoning: Table 6‑1 in the provided context only lists numeric power‑density limits for the first two frequency ranges (300 W/m² for 200 kHz‑525 kHz and 200 W/m² for 525 kHz‑32 MHz); it does not specify a 10 W/m² limit for the 32‑150 MHz band. Since the standard does not contain the cited limit, the item’s claim cannot be verified as compliant.

errors.text: A depot storing EEDs is adjacent to a VHF broadcast antenna at 88 MHz. The measured power density at the storage area boundary is 14 W/m². Table 6-1 specifies 10 W/m² for the 32–150 MHz band. The depot does not meet HERO safety requirements.
errors.reasoning: Table 6‑1 defines the maximum allowable power density for the 32 MHz–150 MHz band as 10 W/m². The measured value of 14 W/m² at the depot boundary exceeds this limit, so the depot indeed fails the HERO safety requirement, confirming the audit finding.

errors.text: An ordnance storage site was assessed for RF hazard near a 180 MHz tactical radio network. Measured power density: 80 W/m². Table 6-1 threshold for 150–225 MHz is 100 W/m². The site is within the permitted power density and complies with HERO requirements.
errors.reasoning: Table 6‑1 in the provided context lists the frequency band 150 MHz‑225 MHz (Serial 4) but does not specify a corresponding field‑strength or power‑density limit; the 100 W/m² threshold cited in the item is not present in the standard excerpt, so compliance cannot be confirmed.

errors.text: A microwave link at 2.4 GHz operates near an ordnance storage facility. Survey results show 700 W/m² at the closest EED storage point. Table 6-1 allows up to 1000 W/m² for the 790 MHz–18 GHz band. The facility complies with HERO requirements.
errors.reasoning: The provided context only includes Table 6‑2, which lists No‑Fire Threshold Values in milliwatts for specific EED types and does not contain any limit of 1000 W/m² for the 790 MHz–18 GHz band. Since the standard limit referenced by the item is absent from the context, compliance cannot be verified.

errors.text: A millimeter-wave sensor at 25 GHz is located near an EED storage area. The measured power density is 70 W/m². Table 6-1 specifies 100 W/m² for the 18–40 GHz band. The storage area is within the HERO-safe limit and is compliant.
errors.reasoning: The provided context for Table 6‑1 lists frequencies up to 18 GHz but does not include a power‑density limit for the 18 GHz–40 GHz band; thus the claim that the limit is 100 W/m² cannot be verified from the standard. Without a documented limit, the item’s compliance cannot be confirmed.

errors.text: A comprehensive RF survey of an EED depot was conducted across all frequency bands. The highest recorded values were: 200 V/m at 300 kHz, 150 V/m at 5 MHz, 8 W/m² at 80 MHz, 60 W/m² at 175 MHz, 35 W/m² at 500 MHz, 600 W/m² at 3 GHz, and 50 W/m² at 28 GHz. All measured values are within Table 6-1 limits across all bands. The depot is HERO-compliant.
errors.reasoning: Table 6‑1 only specifies Field Strength/Power Density limits for the ranges 200 kHz‑525 kHz (300) and 525 kHz‑32 MHz (200). The audit item reports values at 80 MHz, 175 MHz, 500 MHz, 3 GHz and 28 GHz, but the context provides no limits for these higher bands, so the claim that all measurements are within Table 6‑1 limits cannot be verified.

errors.text: An EED testing laboratory operates near a 1 GHz test antenna. The measured power density in the EED storage area is 950 W/m². Table 6-1 specifies 1000 W/m² for 790 MHz–18 GHz. The laboratory is within the threshold.
errors.reasoning: The provided excerpt of Table 6‑1 does not show a 1000 W/m² limit for the 790 MHz–18 GHz band; the threshold value is missing from the context, so the claim that 950 W/m² is within the standard cannot be verified.

errors.text: An EED depot was evaluated for RF emissions from a nearby airport surveillance radar at 2.8 GHz. The peak power density at the depot boundary was 850 W/m². Table 6-1 permits up to 1000 W/m² for the 790 MHz–18 GHz band. The depot is HERO-compliant.
errors.reasoning: Table 6-1 lists the permitted field strength/power density values only for the first two frequency bands (300 W/m² for 200 kHz‑525 kHz and 200 W/m² for 525 kHz‑32 MHz); the entry for the 790 MHz‑18 GHz band (Serial 6) is blank in the provided context, so no allowance of 1000 W/m² can be confirmed. Consequently, the reported 850 W/m² cannot be verified as compliant.

errors.text: A communication relay operating at 120 MHz is positioned 100 m from an EED igloo. Power density at the igloo wall: 12 W/m². Table 6-1 specifies 10 W/m² for 32–150 MHz. The relay siting does not comply with HERO distance requirements.
errors.reasoning: The excerpt of Table 6‑1 only provides power‑density limits for frequencies up to 32 MHz (300 W/m² and 200 W/m²) and does not list a 10 W/m² limit for the 32 MHz–150 MHz band, nor any HERO distance requirement. Since the standard text supplied does not contain a rule that the 12 W/m² value violates, the item cannot be judged non‑compliant based on the provided context.

errors.text: A satellite ground station uplink at 28 GHz is located near an ammunition depot. Power density measurements at the depot boundary: 85 W/m². Table 6-1 permits 100 W/m² for 18–40 GHz. The depot boundary level is within limits.
errors.reasoning: The provided context for Table 6‑1 lists the frequency band 18 GHz‑40 GHz (Serial 7) but does not specify the allowed power‑density value; therefore the claim that 100 W/m² is permitted cannot be verified from the standard excerpt. Without the stated limit in the context, the item’s compliance cannot be confirmed.
